defs: A boat used by rude nations, formed of trunk of a tree, excavated, by cutting of burning, into a suitable shape. It is propelled by a paddle or paddles, or sometimes by sail, and has no rudder.
quotes: Others devised the boat of one tree, called the canoe.

defs: A boat made of bark or skins, used by savages.
quotes: A birch canoe, with paddles, rising, falling, on the water.

defs: A light pleasure boat, especially designed for use by one who goes alone upon long excursions, including portage. It it propelled by a paddle, or by a small sail attached to a temporary mast.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Canoed (k*nd") p. pr. & vb. n. Canoeing (k*n"ng).]

defs: To manage a canoe, or voyage in a canoe.
